title: digitaria dactylon
intro: Digitaria dactylon, also known as Dhaman grass or Cynodon dactylon in some regions, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions and is widely cultivated for its medicinal and edible properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including wound healing, reducing fever, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Digitaria dactylon is often used as a tea or decoction to alleviate various health issues, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, headache, and skin infections.
